🪷 Design your own kurthi in Nepal by @Prasuna.clo ✨ I recently got to experience this beautiful block printing workshop where a plain white kurthi slowly turns into something completely your own. ✨ You get to choose from 3 different kurthi styles, pick your wooden blocks, dip them in color, and print your design one stamp at a time. 🎨🧵 I went all in with a lotus design 🪷, and honestly the whole process was so calming and fun. There’s something really special about watching a blank fabric slowly come to life with your own hands. 🤍 This amazing space was started by Prasuna, who began her journey at just 20 years old. Choosing to stay in Nepal 🇳🇵 and build a business here has made her such an inspiration to so many young people. 🌱 And now every time I wear this kurthi, I’ll remember the experience and the people behind it. 🪷 Watch the video for my final kurthi reveal! 👀✨ #thingstodo #activity #kurthi  #prasunaclo #fyp

💥150 calorie Chicken Summer Rolls   I love Vietnamese food and all the fresh flavors and love making these summer rolls. With the hotter weather coming and seeing @smaller_sam.pcos recently make some, I started craving these again big time. These are super light, fresh, crunchy, and ridiculously satisfying. Just 90 calories each (without sauce) but even better with the peanut sauce below. As always, if you are prioritizing protein, you can swap out the shrimp for things like cooked chicken, tofu, or even steak would work great. Here is how I made them: 🛒 Ingredients (Makes 8 Rolls) -3/4lb chicken thighs seasoned per instructions below) -8 Rice paper sheets (35 cals each, grab them at Asian grocery or online) -A large handful of arugula or thinly sliced gem lettuce  -1 Large red bell pepper, thinly sliced -2 Persian cucumbers, thinly sliced -1/2 large red onion, thinly sliced -1 large avocado, thinly sliced -Large handful of fresh cilantro To make the Chicken: 1.Season 3/4lb chicken thighs with 1/2 tsp avocado oil, 1 tbsp rice vinegar, 1 tbsp soy sauce, salt, pepper, 1 tbsp garlic powder, 1 tbsp onion powder, 1/2 tbsp dried parsley or cilantro, 1/4 tsp cayenne pepper. Mix well. 2. Pop into the oven at 400F for 32 mins, or airfry at 400F for 22 mins.  How to Roll 1.Dip rice paper in warm water (just quick dunk!) and lay it out. 2.Add arugula, then your veggies, leaving space on the edges  3.Fold bottom of rice paper up, pull tight and tuck it in. 4.Add 2-3 ounces of chicken, 2-3 thin avocado slices, and fresh cilantro. 5.Fold in the sides, then roll tight, mini burrito-style. 6.Repeat. This makes 8 rolls depending on how much you fill them up. Optional Spicy Sriracha Sauce 3 tbsp mayo 1 tsp sesame oil 1 tbsp rice vinegar 1 tbsp soy sauce 2.5 tbsp sriracha sauce 1 heaping tsp honey Salt & pepper And if you make them, let me know on SHREDHAPPENS. ENJOY! . . . #lowcarb #lowcarbrecipes #healthyrecipes #healthyfoodshare #goodmoodfood

EASY RICE PAPER TTOEKBOKKI 🥢🇰🇷🌶️ If you’ve never tried chewy rice cakes in a sweet and spicy sauce, this is your call! And it doesn’t get any easier with these DIY rice paper roll ups 🥰 To make this, you’ll need 👉 8-10 rice papers 👉 2 tbsp gochujang paste, or more if you like it 🔥 👉 2 tbsp soy sauce 👉 1 1/2 tbsp sugar 👉 about 200 ml water 🤫 to thicken the sauce, dissolve 1 tsp of cornstarch in 1 tbsp of water FULL RECIPE and instructions in the link in our bio 💛 Follow @kayoukitchen for simple and delicious recipes EVERY WEEK 😋😋😋 #easyrecipe #tteokbokki #ricepapertteokbokki #vegan #veganrecipes #koreanfood #foodtiktok

3 ingredient rice paper banana pancake 🥞🍌 2 rice paper sheets 2 eggs 1 tbsp water 1 banana, sliced Cooking spray or butter, for frying Peanut Butter Drizzle 1 tbsp peanut butter 1 tsp honey 2 tsp melted butter 1. Whisk the eggs and water together in a shallow bowl. 2. Dip one rice paper sheet into the egg mixture until coated, then place into a lightly greased frypan over medium heat. 3. Arrange the banana slices evenly over the rice paper. 4. Dip the second rice paper sheet into the egg mixture and place on top of the banana. 5. Cook for 2-3 minutes, or until golden underneath. Carefully flip and cook for a further 2-3 minutes until golden and crisp. 6. Stir together the peanut butter, honey and melted butter until smooth. 7. Drizzle over the warm banana crepe and serve immediately. #healthyrecipe #breakfast

caption: The dark legend behind this Georgia bridge... Euharlee Covered Bridge looks peaceful now, but there's a darker story locals tell.  🧙‍♀️ In the late 1800s, a witch was traveling through the area and legend says she cursed the well that stood right right in front of the bridge, the water that fed it, and the people of Euharlee.  Another legend refers to something happened on the bridge and to this day you can still hear a rope swaying back and forth and a little girl crying. People report strange experiences here, like sudden cold spots and eerie feeling they can't shake. Some say there's evidence to support these legends. 👻 In the early 2000s, a paranormal team investigated the bridge after hearing the same stories locals had passed down for generations and they decided to test it.  The team closed off the bridge, covered it end to end with sand and stood guard overnight with local residents. No one was allowed to enter. 👣 At dawn, they found boot prints, hoof prints, and wagon wheel tracks crossing the sand. Some believe these were the imprints of trapped souls, still trying to leave Euharlee after all these years. But then again, these are only legends, right?  How about you...would you cross this bridge?👇 📍Euharlee, GA #northgeorgia #georgia #coveredbridge #hauntedplaces #ghoststories

LET ME EXPLAIN-  I have been training all muscle groups in my legs but the problem was the difference in intensity and volume of my workouts. Over time my glute max became overdeveloped since I was able to lift so heavily doing hip thrusts, RDL’s and Bulgarians but i couldn’t lift as heavy doing abductors, kickbacks etc. which caused a big muscle imbalance within my lower body  So because of the over development of my glute max, it became the ONLY muscle in my glutes that got activated during my workouts which is bad because the glute medius was basically not doing anything during my workouts or during everyday activities like walking or standing, causing pain and tightness throughout my lower body.  So the acupuncture was to activate those muscles that weren’t doing their job so I can now properly train them and restore balance between my lower and upper body.  Long story short, even though my glutes look small the muscle itself is still overdeveloped compared to the rest of my small muscles 🥲  #muscleimbalance #gymtok #gymgirl #glutes #acupuncture
